title: salix × sordida var. richteri
intro: Salix × sordida var. richteri, also known as White Willow or Weeping Willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Salicaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to its high salicin content. Salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, has been shown to provide relief from pain, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of rheumatism, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Salix × sordida var. richteri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including menstrual cramps, headaches, and joint pain.
